rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Identify hub genes within co-expression modules

Source: R/modules.R
identify_module_hubs.Rd

Computes within-module centrality for each gene and flags the top-ranked genes as hubs. Optionally maps genes to ortholog groups (HOGs) for downstream conservation analysis with classify_hub_conservation().

identify_module_hubs(modules, ...)

# Default S3 method
identify_module_hubs(
  modules,
  net,
  orthologs = NULL,
  comparison = NULL,
  centrality = c("degree", "betweenness", "eigenvector"),
  top_n = NULL,
  top_fraction = 0.1,
  min_module_size = 3L,
  ...
)

Arguments

modules

Output of detect_modules().

...

Additional arguments passed to the default method.

net

Output of compute_network().

orthologs

Optional data frame from parse_orthologs() with columns Species1, Species2, hog. The function auto-detects which column matches the gene names in modules. If NULL, the hog column in the result is all NA.

comparison

Optional data frame: the $results element from summarize_comparison(). When provided, enables conservation-informed tie-breaking (tiers 5–6). Must contain columns Species1, Species2, hog, Species1.effect.size, Species2.effect.size, plus at least one pair of q-value columns (Species1.q.val.con/Species2.q.val.con or the .div variants).

centrality

Centrality measure: "degree" (default), "betweenness", or "eigenvector".

top_n

Integer: flag the top N genes per module as hubs. If NULL (default), uses top_fraction instead.

top_fraction

Numeric in (0, 1): fraction of genes per module to flag as hubs (default 0.1). Ignored when top_n is non-NULL.

min_module_size

Integer: modules with fewer genes get is_hub = FALSE for all genes (default 3).

Value

A data frame with one row per gene, ordered by module then rank:

gene

Gene identifier

module

Module ID (integer)

degree

Within-module weighted degree (igraph::strength)

betweenness

Within-module betweenness centrality

eigenvector

Within-module eigenvector centrality

mean_edge_weight

Mean weight of edges to other module members

global_degree

Weighted degree in the full (thresholded) network

rank

Rank within module by primary centrality (1 = highest; ties use "min")

is_hub

TRUE if the gene is in the top slice after the 6-tier tie-breaking cascade

hog

HOG identifier (NA if orthologs not provided or gene not in the ortholog table)

Centrality measures

Centrality is computed on the within-module subgraph (edges between genes in the same module only):

degree

Weighted degree (igraph::strength): sum of edge weights to other genes in the same module.

betweenness

Shortest-path betweenness using inverse edge weights as distances. Identifies genes that bridge sub-clusters within a module.

eigenvector

Eigenvector centrality (igraph::eigen_centrality): high for genes connected to other high-centrality genes.

Tie-breaking cascade

When genes share the same primary centrality score, hub selection uses a biologically informed cascade (all available tiers evaluated):

  1. Primary centrality (user-selected measure)

  2. Global weighted degree across the full network

  3. Alternative within-module centrality (betweenness if primary is degree; degree otherwise)

  4. Mean within-module edge weight (strength / degree)

  5. Per-gene conservation effect size (requires comparison)

  6. Per-HOG minimum q-value (requires comparison; lower = better)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
hubs <- identify_module_hubs(modules, net, orthologs,
                             comparison = summary$results)
hubs[hubs$is_hub, ]
} # }

Contents
